title: Reliable Bash Automation and Tests · Command Brief: Bash Strictness
summary: Turn a fragile command history into a bounded script that validates inputs, preserves data, reports failures, and can be rerun safely. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux bash strictness increment in a tested idempotent Bash operations script. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively.
Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A report script receives filenames "north 6.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent. Competency probe: Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively. A defensible solution applies Bash Strictness, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested idempotent Bash operations script.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-bash-strictness — Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively.
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -Eeuo pipefail
tmp=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf -- "$tmp"' EXIT
(( $# > 0 )) || { printf 'usage: %s FILE...\n' "$0" >&2; exit 64; }
printf 'validated=%d\n' "$#"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
